footer.trademarkNotice: Citadel Colour, Vallejo, AK Interactive, and Coat d'Arms are trademarks of their respective owners. coat-codex is an independent fan project and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by any of them.

terms.disclaimerBody: coat-codex is an independent, non-commercial hobby project built by a hobbyist for hobbyists. The developer accepts no responsibility for any loss or corruption of data, or any other damages, arising from use of this app. Backing up your data is your own responsibility.
terms.storageBody: coat-codex stores your recipe data and photos only in your browser's IndexedDB (local storage on your device). Nothing is sent to or stored on an external server. As a result, your data can be lost if you clear browser data, uninstall the app, or reset your device.
terms.safariRiskBody: Due to Safari's Intelligent Tracking Prevention (ITP), your saved data may be automatically deleted if coat-codex is not opened for 7 consecutive days. We recommend opening the app regularly, or backing up your data as described below.
terms.backupBody: Regularly back up your important recipes using each recipe's JSON export feature. Exported JSON files can be restored via the import feature, on the same device or a different one.
terms.trademarkIntro: coat-codex is an independent, non-commercial hobby project built by a hobbyist for hobbyists. It is not affiliated with, endorsed by, sponsored by, or otherwise officially connected with any of the paint manufacturers or brands referenced within the app.
terms.trademarkListIntro: The following brand and product names referenced in coat-codex are trademarks or trade names of their respective owners:
terms.trademarkCitadel: Citadel Colour — a trademark of Games Workshop Limited
terms.trademarkVallejo: Vallejo — a trademark of Acrylicos Vallejo, S.L.
terms.trademarkAk: AK Interactive — a trademark of its manufacturer
terms.trademarkCoatDarms: Coat d'Arms — a trade name of its current manufacturer/distributor
terms.trademarkOwnership: All product names, brand names, and trademarks referenced here remain the property of their respective owners. They are used in coat-codex solely to help users identify, record, and reference paint colours for their own personal painting logs — not to imply any business relationship, sponsorship, or endorsement.
terms.trademarkColorNote: Colour values (hex/RGB) shown in coat-codex are digital approximations of physical paint colours, compiled from publicly available reference sources. Actual paint colour may vary due to screen calibration, lighting conditions, and manufacturing batch variation.
terms.trademarkContact: If you are a rights holder and have a concern about how your brand is referenced here, please contact: {{email}}

help.title: HOW TO USE
help.gloss: How to use coat-codex and frequently asked questions
help.intro: Here's a step-by-step overview of how to use coat-codex, from creating a recipe to sharing and printing it.

create.title: 1. Create a recipe
create.body: Start from "New Recipe" on Home. A title alone is enough to begin — you can fill in the rest later.
create.imageAlt: The new recipe screen shown right after pressing "New Recipe"

setup.title: 2. Set up the basics
setup.body: Register the title, a photo of the finished piece, and the tools used. The finished photo can be added later, so you can start even mid-project.
setup.imageAlt: Screen for registering a recipe's basic information

editor.title: 3. Edit the steps
editor.body: For each part or the base, record the technique, paint (search presets from 3 brands or enter your own), mix ratio, notes, and step photos. Add as many steps as you like.
editor.imageAlt: Screen for editing a part's steps

overview.title: 4. Manage from the overview
overview.body: Reorder BASE and PARTS cards, review steps, and change the overview photo. See the whole recipe at a glance while you organize it.
overview.imageAlt: Overview screen managing BASE and PARTS cards

share.title: 5. Share to social media
share.body: Choose "Post to Social" from "Export & Share". Switch between X and Bluesky, and pick up to 4 share card images.
share.imageAlt: Social share screen for selecting a target and images

print.title: 6. Print or save as PDF
print.body: Print your recipe from the print view. To save as a PDF, choose "Save as PDF" in your browser's print dialog.
print.imageAlt: Print view screen displaying a recipe

help.backupBody: Export a recipe as JSON (with photos included) from its card menu on Home. To restore, use "Import JSON" on Home.

dataWhere.a: Only in this device's browser (IndexedDB). Nothing is sent to a server, and no account is required.

dataLost.a: Yes — clearing site data in your browser or using private browsing can erase it. If storage protection hasn't been granted, the browser may also delete data at its own discretion when storage is low or after a long period without a visit (around 7 days on Safari). We recommend backing up regularly via JSON export.

backupRestore.q: How do I back up and restore?
backupRestore.a: Use "Export JSON" from a recipe's card menu on Home to back up (photos included). To restore, use "Import JSON" on Home.

moveDevice.q: Can I move my data to another device?
moveDevice.a: Move the exported JSON file to the other device and import it there.

supportedBrowsers.a: The latest versions of Chrome, Edge, Safari, and Firefox. On Safari, version 16.2 or later is recommended. Both smartphones and PCs are supported.

offline.a: Loading the page requires an internet connection. The data you've saved, however, lives on your device.

shareCard.a: Images automatically generated from your recipe for posting (a full summary or per-part). You can select up to 4. On environments without share-sheet support, save the images and attach them manually in the posting screen.

pdf.q: How do I save as a PDF?
pdf.a: Choose "Print", then select "Save as PDF" in your browser's print dialog.

noteMd.q: What is the note Markdown feature?
noteMd.a: A feature that copies your full recipe in a format you can paste directly into a note article.

tools.title: Tool Library
tools.description: Manage your tools locally on this device. Deleting a tool from the library does not affect saved recipes.
tools.emptyDescription: Enter a tool name to add one.
tools.deleteMessage: This will not be removed from saved recipes.
